name: Port Louis
summary: Mauritius is a sunny island nation in the Indian Ocean, east of Madagascar, with green mountains poking up like crinkled hats. It is famous as the only home of the dodo, a chunky flightless bird that vanished long ago. People here speak French, English and Mauritian Creole, and celebrate Hindu, Christian, Muslim and Chinese festivals all on one small island.
funFacts: The dodo lived only on Mauritius and was wiped out by the 1680s after sailors and the dogs, pigs and rats they brought hunted them.
Mauritian Creole mixes French with words from Africa, India and Madagascar, so 'how are you?' becomes 'ki manyer?' instead of a French sentence.
Sugar cane covers huge fields across Mauritius because British rulers brought half a million workers from India in the 1800s to grow it.
At Chamarel you can see sand in seven natural colours, red, brown, violet, green, blue, purple and yellow, made by old volcanic rock.
Mauritius is the only African country where Hinduism is the biggest religion, but Christian, Muslim and Buddhist festivals are also national holidays.
thingsToDo: Walk straight off the ship into Le Caudan Waterfront for shops, snacks and a sea-facing playground only minutes from the cruise terminal.
Visit the Blue Penny Museum at Caudan to glimpse two of the world's rarest stamps, shown for just ten minutes each hour.
Wander the Central Market near Place d'Armes to sniff vanilla, chillies and spices and try fresh pineapple sliced into crinkly stars.
Hike up to the Citadel (Fort Adelaide) above the city for a breezy view over the harbour, racetrack and pointy Pieter Both mountain.
Taxi to Pamplemousses Botanical Garden to spot giant Victoria water lilies, big enough to hold a small child, and slow-moving giant tortoises.
Step inside the Natural History Museum at the Mauritius Institute to meet a reconstructed dodo skeleton and learn how the bird lived.
